name: Aaktay
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together in a bowl all the dry ingredients including dates and walnuts. Leave out the buttermilk, honey, orange half, and rhubarb.
2. In a blender, mix the buttermilk and honey. Cut an unpeeled orange in half and remove any labels or marks. Chop the orange into pieces and add to the blender. Blend until smooth.
3. Add the blended mixture (buttermilk and honey) to the dry ingredients and mix well until combined.
4. Grease empty cans with margarine.
5. Pour the batter into the greased cans, filling each no more than two-thirds full. Cover the filled cans with aluminum foil and secure with rubber bands.
6. Place the cans in a large pot on a rack so they are not touching the bottom of the pot. Fill the pot with hot water until the bottom 2 inches of the cans are covered.
7. Bring to a boil and maintain gentle boiling for about 1 1/2 hours, checking occasionally to ensure there is enough water.
8. After 1 1/2 hours, check the doneness by piercing through the foil with a skewer. If it comes out clean, remove the hot cans from the pot. When ready to serve, uncover the loaf, place on a breadboard, and slice. Serve warm with butter or cream cheese.
